outlook: UX Researcher roles are expanding with 2,729 open positions representing an 8.3% increase from last year, though the $73,000 median salary suggests the field remains cost-conscious about research investments. The profession faces moderate AI disruption with a 29/100 risk score, as synthetic users and AI interview summarizers begin automating parts of traditional user research workflows. Companies are prioritizing researchers who excel at active listening, critical thinking, and reading comprehension—the distinctly human skills that interpret user behavior and extract meaningful insights from complex data patterns. The decline in demand for technical skills like business intelligence software and word processing indicates the role is shifting away from administrative tasks toward higher-level strategic thinking and stakeholder communication.

skillPivots.headline: Develop advanced active listening frameworks
skillPivots.body: Create structured methodologies for capturing nuanced user insights during interviews and usability sessions. Build expertise in techniques like reflective listening and emotional mapping to differentiate from AI interview summarizers.

skillPivots.headline: Master critical thinking for research strategy
skillPivots.body: Pivot from basic data analysis to strategic research planning and hypothesis formation. Focus on designing research frameworks that challenge assumptions and guide product decisions beyond what synthetic users can provide.

skillPivots.headline: Build reading comprehension for cross-functional collaboration
skillPivots.body: Develop skills in translating complex user research findings into actionable insights for engineering, marketing, and business teams. Create clear documentation and presentation formats that make research accessible across disciplines.
